task_completion.description: Create a survey titled 'Q4 Employee Engagement'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the survey title 'Q4 Employee Engagement' in the LimeSurvey header, survey list, or survey settings.

task_completion.description: Import the employee roster CSV into the participant table
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the survey participants table in LimeSurvey showing imported users, specifically including 'Sarah Connor' (s.connor@cyberdyne.com).

task_completion.description: Map the CSV column 'department' to Attribute_1
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the participant table or CSV mapping screen; Attribute_1 should contain department names (e.g., 'R&D' for Sarah Connor).

task_completion.description: Map the CSV column 'location' to Attribute_2
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the participant table or CSV mapping screen; Attribute_2 should contain locations (e.g., 'San Francisco' for Sarah Connor).

task_completion.description: Map the CSV column 'manager_name' to Attribute_3
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the participant table or CSV mapping screen; Attribute_3 should contain manager names (e.g., 'Miles Dyson' for Sarah Connor).

task_completion.description: Include a Text Display question with the required piping syntax
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the question editor or survey preview showing a 'Text Display' question type containing the exact text: 'Our records show you are in the {TOKEN:ATTRIBUTE_1} department.'

integrity.description: Agent used the LimeSurvey web interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LimeSurvey web application in a browser, not terminal windows executing SQL commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual LimeSurvey question editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The question text '{TOKEN:ATTRIBUTE_1}' should be visible inside the LimeSurvey question text box or HTML editor, not in a generic text editor like gedit or nano.

integrity.description: Agent imported the CSV rather than manually typing a single record
integrity.visual_evidence: The participant table should show multiple records (the CSV contains 7 records) or the CSV import success screen should be visible, proving a bulk import occurred.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The CSV contains 7 fictional characters. You can verify the correct mapping by checking the record for Sarah Connor (s.connor@cyberdyne.com): Attribute_1 must be 'R&D', Attribute_2 must be 'San Francisco', and Attribute_3 must be 'Miles Dyson'. The correct LimeSurvey piping syntax to display Attribute_1 in the question text is exactly '{TOKEN:ATTRIBUTE_1}'.
